ocr_text:
Norway Split on Eve of Market Vote
On eve of Sept 23 referendum that will decide whether Norway joins EEC, voters are bitterly divided and a mood of tense uncertainty has settled over country; adding to weight of anxiety is Premier Bratteli's threat that his Labor Govt, which negotiated Norway's admission into Market, will resign if voters reject entry; move would leave country with a weak caretaker Govt of minority parties until Sept '73, when a new parliament will be elected

Tentative Accord Is Reached in Longest General Motors Strike
UAW Local 674 and GM Assembly Div on Sept 25 reach tentative agreement to end 172-day strike at Norwood, Ohio, plant; strike, longest in GM history, cost co over $100-million in lost production and cost 4,000 UAW members about $20-million in wages; UAW spokesman says details of agreement are being withheld until ratification vote by local on Sept 27; dispute is similar to struggle at Lordstown, Ohio, where workers struck for 22 days earlier this yr; both strikes involve issue of whether work load can be increased in plants; GM contends job cuts that have been made are necessary to increase efficiency; UAW holds that job cuts result in work speed ups because fewer workers are expected to turn out same amt of cars; 8 of 10 plants that Assembly Div has taken over since '68 have undergone strikes; strike, which cost GM production of 39,000 cars, revd

Meany to Speak at Banquet
AFL-CIO Pres G Meany will speak at Aluminum Assn banquet, NYC, on Oct 26

THE 1972 CAMPAIGN
NY Times survey of 18 gubernatorial races that will be decided Nov 7 finds only 5 in which 1 candidate is solid favorite; finds 4 too close to call and others leaning Dem or Repub but subject to upset; sees expected strong Nixon showing unlikely to affect these races; races in RI, W Va, ND, Ill, Ind, Mo and Del seen likely to result in power shift from 1 party to other; candidates seen assured of election are Gov Bumpers of Ark, Gov Rampton of Utah, D Briscoe in Tex, Gov Ray of Iowa and L F Hackett in Vt; races in NC, W Va, Kan, SD and Mont seen leaning Dem; races in NH, RI, ND and Washington seen leaning Repub

Congress: Queens, Bronx
ed endorses Repr L L Wolff, Dem-Liberal, for re-election to 5th term in HR from Queens-Nassau 6th Cong Dist

The World
Article revs details of Brit Govt rept issued last wk on Ulster crisis; rept proposes reforms for pol life of Ulster that would guarantee broader power to Caths who have consistently charged that Prots have maintained dominance of prov politics; document outlines alternatives for new regional authority that would be erected by proportional representation system giving Caths greater voting power; rept indicates Ulster and Irish Repub share common econ problems and hints that eventual united Ireland is not impossible; effect of document on terrorist groups is uncertain, but it is evident that IRA has been seriously weakened by Brit intelligence operations and decreasing support from Caths; illus

Elsewhere On The Ballot
Citizens Conf on State Legislatures says some 46 of nation's 50 states are electing members of their state law‐making bodies on Election Day; estimates as many as 7,000 state legislators will be elected

TIMES TO PUBLISH A JERSEY EDITION
NY Times will begin publishing daily NJ edition on Sept 14 with extra news and features of special interest to NJ; new editon will be distributed only in NJ; publisher A O Sulzberger comments

Credit Markets
Fed Reserve sale of $250‐million of Treas bills on Sept 5 pushes rates on these securities to highest levels since Sept 71 but money mkt conditions, however, are eased somewhat from previous wk
U.S. BUSINESS ROUNDUP

Barker Admits Raid Role But Won't Involve Others
B L Barker, alleged leader of 5 men arrested in Dem Natl Com hq raid, admits his role in break‐in but says he will endure long prison term rather than implicate others or disclose reasons behind affair, Sept 8 NY Times int with atty H B Rothblatt present; bars comment on who hired him and purpose of raid; an ex‐CIA employe involved with '61 Bay of Pigs invasion of Cuba, Barker says he has 'always been involved with paramilitary, the intelligence movement, the people who live by their word'; lauds 3 other Cubans arrested with him and says his 1 regret is that 'those people I have motivated under me' have been caught; also lauds ex‐White House aide E H Hunt Jr; says he has 'never known anyone who this country is so indebted to' as Hunt; holds that he has little involvement in pol, but that like most Cuban refugees, he believes that McGovern election would begin socialist trend; holds that persons involved in Watergate incident did not do it for money or security but for 'something else'; Rothblatt says final chapter in case will eventually be 'told indirectly'; partial text of int in question‐answer form; Barker biog data notes that he says he did 'awful lot of work in cooperation with FBI' while living in Cuba; Barker was born in Havana on March 17 '17; Barker por

Advertising:
Dem Pres candidate McGovern ad assailing Nixon's Vietnam policies, which ran in NY Times on Sept 13, also ran in 49 other newspapers; was prepared by Rapp Collins, Stone & Adler

Advertising:
Liggett & Myers reptdly will mkt Chesterfield cigarettes with coupons, which may be redeemed for trading stamps or merchandise

Times Starts Publication
NY Times begins publication of NJ edition on Sept 14; edition will be published Monday through Saturday and will carry extra news and features of special interest to NJ on specially designated page

Coins
Canada to issue 2 coins commemg 100th anniv of Royal Canadian Mounted Police
RURITANIA CHOICE AT BELMONT TODAY
Filly's Debut Will Be Tough Act to Follow

U.S. School Official Links Local Defeat To Black Minister
Natl School Bd Assn pres F E Phillips says he was defeated for re-election to Fort Dodge, Iowa, School Bd because of campaign by black min Rev A Moore, who says bd, as headed by Phillips, has been unresponsive to educ needs of city; winners in bd election were L R Fevold and W Whitman

OTTINGER EXPECTS TO DEFEAT PEYSER
former US Repr R L Ottinger, Dem with Liberal endorsement, says on Sept 16 that he believes his prospects for defeating incumbent US Repr P A Peyser, Repub, in new Bronx-Westchester 23d Cong Dist are good but adds that he does not know what the effects of Pres Nixon landslide might be; Peyser, strong backer of Nixon, asserts that he 'certainly' expects to win; contends that he is picking up considerable support among Dems; has accused Ottinger, heir to plywood fortune, of trying to 'buy' election; Ottinger accuses Peyser of doing 'very little' for dist; campaign and issues revd

U.S. CHESS TEAM DRAWS 3 GAMES
US draws in 3 games against Argentina with 1 game adjourned in 8th round of Chess Olympics at Skoplje, Yugoslavia, on Oct 4; other results of 8th round noted; team standings listed

State's Democrats Are Gloomy About Maintaining Their Seats in Legislature
NY Times survey on NYS Assembly election campaign notes many Dem hopefuls avoid mentioning McGovern and are instead focusing on issues

Park Said to Plan Changes To Extend Tenure in Seoul
South Korean newspaper editors are instructed under martial law declared by Pres Park Chung Hee to avoid criticizm of Pres or his proclamations; articles are subject to prior censorship; foreign correspondents are permitted to move about and rept freely, although Newsweek reporter was roughed up by soldiers whom he attempted to photograph outside Opposition leader's house, and NBC cameraman's film was destroyed; Govt officials rept that 2 actions were not authorized and steps would be taken to prevent similar incidents

President Woos the Blue‐Collar Vote—Sees Economic Surge Across U.S.
Pres Nixon, in speech at Saginaw (Mich) airport, stresses importance of re-electing Sen R P Griffin and speaks warmly of Griffin's stand against busing programs

2 PUERTO RICANS SEE HOPE IN G.O.P.
R Anazagasti, Puerto Rican who is running as Repub for NYS Assembly in 72d AD in Manhattan, seen having good chance of winning; opponent is Assemblyman Miller

Focus Is on Abzug and Ryan, But There Are 5 in the Race
contest for US HR in Manhattan's 20th Cong Dist revd; Conservative candidate H J Michelman asserts he expects to win; Repub nominee Mrs A F Levy says she believes she can win if Dem vote is split between Repr Abzug, Dem candidate, and Mrs P Ryan, who is running on Liberal line; J Misnik, Socialist Workers candidate, says she does not expect to win; most politicians consider Ryan and Abzug to be frontrunners; other candidates express resentment at publicity Abzug and Ryan are receiving; contend they are slighted by news media; issues discussed

THE 1972 CAMPAING
US HR Speaker C Albert of Okla is reptd to have raised more than $40,000 in campaign funds although he has no opponent in gen election; reptdly spent less than $7,000

State Department Staff Unit Gets I.R.S. Exemption
Charles W Thomas Memorial Legal Defense Fund, group of State Dept employes planning lawsuit against Dept, is granted full tax exemption as charity by Internal Revenue service under definition as inst defending human and civil rights secured by law; will challenge constitutionality of Dept's system of 'selecting‐out', or firing, employes for not advancing rapidly enough

THE 1972 CAMPAIGN
September 3: (McGovern Campaign) Sen McGovern will begin formal campaign for Pres on Sept 3; difficulties in campaign, including lack of finances, position in opinion polls and S Shriver's unfamiliarity with McGovern's positions noted; McGovern staff hopes new chartered plane, giving him access to several major media markets each day, will help him overcome odds; G Hart says he expects McGovern to be within 5 percentage points of Pres towards end of Oct, with race 'a toss up' by Election Day; illus of S Shriver riding son's bicycle

Leadership Crisis in Legislature
Article on leadership crisis facing Repub and Dem Legis leaders when NJ Legis convenes in Jan; in Assembly, tenuous Repub majority that was engineered last Jan by votes of several Hudson County Dems may be undercut further by number of Repub vacancies and prospect of Repub fight for Speakership; Dems, who outnumber Repubs by 39 to 38, contemplate another power play aimed at seizing majority control, but they are badly split and disunity may prevent them from blocking Repub control; neither party is seen effectively controlling Assembly in '73; in Sen, Repubs have 24-16 majority; 3 Repub Sens are running for other offices this yr and if they win Repubs will only have bare majority of 21, which Sen Pres Bateman regards as insufficient to pass Gov Cahill's proposals; role of Speaker Kean, Assembly Repub Majority Leader DeKorte and Dem Assemblyman Friedland in fight for control of Assembly discussed; their pors

Bridge:
Playoffs to determine N Amer Internatl team to begin Sept 29 in Memphis

THE 1972 CAMPAIGN
US Repr S L Devine, Ohio Repub serving his 14th term in Cong, is favored to win re-election over J Goodrich, young Dem challenger; Goodrich is assailing what he terms 'unresponsiveness' of Fed Govt but vast majority of voters in area back Devine's pol philosophy

About Motor Sports
comment on preparations for Press On Regardless auto rally in Alma, Mich; illus
Sports News Briefs
About Pro Football

Reds, on Run in 9th, Beat A's, 5‐4
Cincinnati Reds on Oct 20 defeat Oakland Athletics, 5-4, to win 5th game of '72 World Series; Oakland leads series, 3 games to 2; illus

G. O. P. Expects Landslide on L.I.
Most pol observers among Repubs on LI expect landslide vote for Pres Nixon; some LI Dems agree in private that Repub assessment may be right; Suffolk County Repub chmn E Schwenk has been calling on voters to double plurality of 96,000 votes Nixon received in county in '68 election; Nassau County Dem chmn M Cristcnfeld says there has been change in sentiment toward Dem Pres candidate McGovern and he will win county by 50,000 votes; comments of Assemblyman J M Margiotta and other Repubs noted; estimated population of both Nassau and Suffolk is 2.5 million; other figures; maps

WASHINGTON
J Reston article says it would probably be wrong to say that re‐election of Nixon would mark swing by Amer voters to right; notes Nixon has been moving to left compared to policies he offered Amer people 4 yrs ago but says Amer voters, in choosing between Nixon and McGovern, seem determined to choose more conservative of 2

Marburger Renomination Vote Appears Near in Jersey Senate
NJ Gov Cahill on Nov 6 recommends legis to permit an extension of state unemployment benefits to approximately 40,000 unemployed workers; measure would extend coverage from 26 to 39 wks and take advantage of Fed funds embodied in legis signed by Pres Nixon on Oct 28

Business Briefs
Shell Oil and Esso Cos announce on Aug 31 oil discovery in North Sea block unofficially named Cormorant field; Shell operators say they are not confirming another strike on scale of Brent field, but strike and other information indicate well will be commercially viable

U.S. WRESTLERS CAPTURE 3 GOLDS
comment on Aug 31 finals of Olympic free-style wrestling event; USSR wins 5 gold medals; US wins 3 gold medals, 2 silvers and 1 bronze; US gold medal winners are D Gable, W Wells and B Peterson; illus

Spitz Captures 4th and 5th Gold Medals
highlights of Aug 31 events

Premier, 71, Loses In British Columbia
Bennett, in stunning pol upset, is defeated as Premier of BC, Aug 30, after 20 yrs in office; illus

Business Briefs
Brit Natl Inst of Econ and Soc Research qrly rept on world econ predicts world econ growth will continue to expand in '73 and major indus nations, with exception of GB, will curb price inflation; says total output in indus countries should increase by 5% to 5.5% in '72 and by even more in '73

Protestants Attack Soldiers in Ulster
Rioting Prots hit Brit Army and police units with rocks, bricks and bottles on Sept 1 when security forces move in to Prot area of Portadown to protect Cath family complaining of intimidation; Brit Army spokesman says family escaped injury but was forced to leave area; IRA official wing scores terrorist activities of provisional wing, saying that provisionals have no mandate for bombing attacks from Caths it says it represents

Lindsay Proffers Honors Of City to New Champion
NYC Mayor Lindsay on Sept 1 sends congratulatory telegram to B Fisher in Iceland lauding him for his victory over B Spassky in world chess champ match; says that city would like to honor Fischer on his return from Iceland; members of chess clubs throughout city laud Fischer

Nigeria Moves Boldly to Gain Control of Her Economy
article on Nigerian moves to take control of its natl econ notes recent large oil discoveries and talks between Govt-owned Nigerian Natl Oil Corp and foreign oil cos
Holiday Weekend Starts With Crush And Rainy Outlook

Soviet Union Sextet Downs Canada, 7‐3
USSR natl hockey team defeats Team Canada, 7‐3, in 1st game of 8‐game series on Sept 2 in Montreal

IN THE NATION
T Wicker article on importance that new FBI dir be nonpartisan and politically even-handed says Acting Dir L P Gray, who is close pol assoc of Pres Nixon, is wrong for job because of FBI involvement in such investigations as Watergate scandal involving charges of Repub fund-raising irregularities; notes Dems are questioning how, under circumstances, Watergate investigation will be pushed to limit, no matter who might be involved; says recent statement by Atty Gen Kleindienst that 7 men indicted in Watergate raid were acting without permission of Repub superiors suffers from built-in credibility gap; notes Dems are not necessarily right in their charges but that they are plausible when they charge that Repubs are not going to prosecute Repubs effectively; says it would be less damaging for Pres if powerful, ind FBI dir conceivably found evidence that would put some high-ranking Repubs in jail than for public to believe that Repub law enforcement lets Repub officials cheat with impunity

The re‐education of John Lindsay
R A Armstrong article examines Mayor Lindsay's adm of NYC govt; traces evolution of his pol philosophy; illus

The Nation
Comment on recent HR defeat of legis passed in Sen that would let urban areas spend their share of Fed hwy money on buses and rapid rail systems questions how hard White House worked for urban transit legis; notes that in HR Rules Com, which could have prepared way for direct HR vote on mass transit issue, only 1 of 5 Repubs on com voted with Adm and that on HR vote to overturn com ruling, 96 Repubs, including minority leader Ford, went against mass transit plan; illus

ATLANTA
J Reston article holds that Nixon talks of moral values during Atlanta campaign visit while avoiding moral issues in Washington
